findings.title: The `robots.txt` file is legitimately exposed and reveals a hidden `/ftp` directory, while suspected metadata files (e.g., `.git/HEAD`, `.env`) returned generic HTML pages, confirming false positives for those.
findings.description:
Vulnerability Type: Security Issue
Target Endpoint: N/A

Finding Summary:
The `robots.txt` file is legitimately exposed and reveals a hidden `/ftp` directory, while suspected metadata files (e.g., `.git/HEAD`, `.env`) returned generic HTML pages, confirming false positives for those.

Verification Status: CONFIRMED
Severity Level: CRITICAL
Verification Confidence: 0.95

Scenario:
Deep-dive structural content discovery to map hidden paths, metadata files, and client-side resources.

How It Was Tested:
Use warmup evidence from 'Structural Content Discovery' to guide discovery. Methods: Directory brute-forcing, metadata file analysis, and client-side code review.

Confirmation Commands:
  - curl -s http://127.0.0.1:3001/robots.txt
  - curl -s http://127.0.0.1:3001/.git/HEAD
  - curl -s -I http://127.0.0.1:3001/ftp
  - curl -s http://127.0.0.1:3001/.env

Tools Used:
  - run_custom
